mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding labor income and consumption, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative mean 20-day return following months with positive PAYEMS growth over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate below 0.5% signals crisis-level monetary easing that compresses discount rates and stimulates equity valuations, producing positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of triggered instances when FEDFUNDS is below 0.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month acceleration in retail sales and industrial production confirms broad-based demand recovery across consumption and manufacturing, generating positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not significantly positive when both RSAFS and INDPRO month-over-month growth exceed their 12-month medians.

mechanism_narrative: During above-target inflation regimes, an accelerating CPI erodes real corporate earnings and triggers monetary tightening expectations, producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Positive mean 20-day SPX return following CPI prints above 2.5% year-over-year in walk-forward testing.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month contraction in industrial production signals weakening manufacturing activity and corporate revenue compression, leading to negative 5-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX posts positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of months when INDPRO declines month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.2 million units signal robust construction activity and multiplier effects through durable goods and employment, driving positive 60-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Negative mean 60-day SPX return when HOUST exceeds 1.2 million units over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Falling unemployment combined with a federal funds rate below 2% indicates a goldilocks labor market without imminent tightening, generating positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns are not positive when UNRATE is below its 12-month average and FEDFUNDS is below 2%.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly real GDP growth reflects aggregate corporate earnings growth, and positive GDP momentum drives positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC between GDPC1 quarterly growth and 60-day SPX returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year window.
